steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank comes after "an array of ______" and is followed by three semicolon-separated examples (the Sarulla plant; the Reykjanes plant; the McGinness Hills plant). The blank must introduce that list while keeping "in Indonesia" as a location modifier for the first example, matching the pattern "in Iceland" and "in the US" used in the other two.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• "technologies, in Indonesia,": A comma after "technologies" joins two independent clauses with only a comma — a comma splice. ✗
  • "technologies: in Indonesia,": The colon after "technologies" correctly introduces the series of examples. "in Indonesia," then serves as a location modifier for the Sarulla plant, parallel to "in Iceland" and "in the US." ✓
  • "technologies in Indonesia:": Placing the colon after "Indonesia" restricts the colon to introducing the Sarulla example only and breaks the parallel structure with the other two examples. ✗
  • "technologies in Indonesia;": The semicolon can separate independent clauses, but this version attaches "in Indonesia" to "technologies," incorrectly suggesting the world's geothermal plants leverage technologies located in Indonesia instead of introducing a three-example list. ✗
That matches the correct answer — B is correct.
